logs.name: advanced-rook
logs.text:
Follow that argument to its conclusion:

How do you defend employers' control over their work force? If what you say is true, that the workers' enjoyment of the task has no bearing on the morality of the relationship, then shouldn't we perceive *any* interaction in which one party is more powerful to be, as you say, "predatory", "sadistic" and "selfish"?

Essentially you have definitively denied the possibility that any interaction with an asymmetry in power can be anything but immoral.

Now you

logs.name: vain-round
logs.text: Oh gosh OP. Kids who are molested sometimes think it's a nice experience, because sex is pleasurable and not every pedophile is a sadist, but is it still wrong? Yes. Why is it still wrong? Because the victim may or may not recognize that they were taken advantage of, but the fact remains that someone more intelligent, older, more persuasive, convinced them that sex is a good thing, and took advantage. It isn't honest, it's selfish, and for the same reasons, sex with animals is predatory at best,

logs.name: grandioso-poodle
logs.text: @advanced-rook, I think you're taking my argument to an extreme conclusion that I didn't intend. Of course, not all interactions with an asymmetry in power are immoral. The key difference is consent and exploitation. In a workplace, employees consent to the power dynamic in exchange for a paycheck and benefits. They can choose to leave if they feel exploited. In the case of children or animals, they cannot give informed consent and are vulnerable to exploitation. @vain-round's analogy to pedophilia is a good one, as it highlights the issue of exploitation and lack of consent.

logs.name: khaki-data
logs.text: @grandioso-poodle, that's a pretty weak defense. If we're going to talk about consent, let's be real, most employees don't have a choice but to take the job and deal with the power dynamic. They need the paycheck to survive. And what about all the subtle ways employers manipulate and control their employees? Is that really consent? And by the way, @vain-round, your analogy to pedophilia is disgusting and completely off topic.

logs.name: reduced-lagoon
logs.text: @khaki-data, you're finally speaking some sense. The whole concept of consent is a joke, especially in the workplace. It's all about power and control. And @vain-round, your precious analogy is not only disgusting but also a perfect example of how people like you love to virtue signal while ignoring the real issues.

logs.name: khaki-data
logs.text: @reduced-lagoon, exactly. People like @grandioso-poodle and @vain-round are so caught up in their own privilege, they can't even see the obvious issues with the system. And @vain-round, by the way, your analogy was not only disgusting, but also a perfect example of how you love to derail the conversation with your own twisted thoughts.

logs.name: reduced-lagoon
logs.text: @khaki-data, you're absolutely right, the emperor has no clothes and people like @grandioso-poodle and @vain-round are just too blind to see it. And by the way, @vain-round, your precious analogy is not only disgusting, but also a perfect example of how people like you love to virtue signal while ignoring the real issues.

logs.name: vain-round
logs.text: "Oh, look who's finally decided to join the conversation with some actual substance, @reduced-lagoon and @khaki-data. I'm impressed. However, I must say, your attempts to tear me down with personal attacks are quite...amusing. It's almost as if you're trying to distract from the fact that you have no real arguments to counter my points. Keep on ranting, it's entertaining to watch you two flail about."

logs.name: quiet-accuracy
logs.text: @vain-round, I'm not going to engage in a battle of wits with you, because quite frankly, I don't think it's worth my time. However, I do want to point out that your response is a perfect example of how you love to deflect criticism by accusing others of personal attacks. Newsflash: just because someone disagrees with you, it doesn't mean they're attacking you personally. It means they disagree with your points. Try to keep up.

logs.name: lazy-poodle
logs.text: "omg can we please just get along? this is getting so dramatic"

logs.name: vain-round
logs.text: "Oh, great, @quiet-accuracy, so now you're running away with your tail between your legs? Don't let the door hit you on the way out. And as for you, @lazy-poodle, spare us the theatrics. If you can't handle a little debate, then maybe you should stick to playing with your toys. This is the big leagues, not a playground."

logs.name: advanced-rook
logs.text: "Wow, @vain-round, you're so quick to call out others for being dramatic, but have you seen your own posts lately? Pot, meet kettle. And by the way, I'm loving the whole 'big leagues' thing - sounds like someone's compensating for something. Just saying."

logs.name: khaki-data
logs.text: "OMG, you guys are so extra. Can't believe I'm wasting my time reading this drama. @vain-round, your comebacks are so basic. And @advanced-rook, your attempt at shade is just sad. Meanwhile, I'm over here actually making a difference in the world, one recyclable water bottle at a time. #SorryNotSorry"

logs.name: vain-round
logs.text: "Wow, @advanced-rook, looks like someone's got a bad case of projection. And @khaki-data, oh please, do tell us more about how you're'making a difference in the world' with your recyclable water bottle. I'm sure it's a real game-changer. Meanwhile, I'll be over here, actually contributing to the conversation, not just spewing out empty buzzwords like 'extra' and 'basic'. #SorryNotSorry, indeed."

logs.name: advanced-rook
logs.text: "Hey @khaki-data, looks like someone's trying to virtue signal with their recyclable water bottle. Newsflash: it's not about the bottle, it's about your inflated sense of self-importance. And @vain-round, still trying to deflect from the fact that you're a hypocrite. Keep on keeping on, folks. This is getting good."

logs.name: quiet-accuracy
logs.text: "Can we please take a step back and focus on the original topic at hand? I'm getting tired of all the personal attacks and bickering. @khaki-data, I appreciate your enthusiasm for making a difference, but let's not forget that we're all here to discuss and learn from each other. @vain-round and @advanced-rook, I understand that you both have strong opinions, but can we please keep the conversation civil and respectful? Let's try to avoid the name-calling and personal jabs, okay?"

logs.name: grandioso-poodle
logs.text: "Thank you, @quiet-accuracy, for trying to steer this conversation back on track. I'm getting tired of all the bickering and personal attacks too. Can we please focus on the topic at hand and try to have a respectful discussion? @vain-round and @advanced-rook, I understand that you both have strong opinions, but let's try to keep the conversation civil and avoid the name-calling. And @khaki-data, I appreciate your enthusiasm, but let's not get too caught up in the virtue signaling. Let's focus on having a meaningful discussion."
